---
title: "Flatstem sida (Sida planicaulis)"
slug: "sida-planicaulis"
group: "Plantae"
image: "https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/475170656/medium.jpg"        # keep for thumbnails
image-alt: "Flatstem sida"  # keep for a11y
tax_kingdom: "Plantae"
tax_class: "Magnoliopsida"
tax_order: "Malvales"
tax_family: "Malvaceae"
categories: ["Plantae", "Magnoliopsida", "Malvales", "Malvaceae"]   # these drive the chips + filtering
freeze: true
---

<figure class="species-figure">

<img src="https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/475170656/medium.jpg" alt="Flatstem sida" class="species-photo"/>

<figcaption class="photo-credit">

Photo: <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/people/damien6868">
damien6868</a> (CC BY) via <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/observations/264614273">iNaturalist</a>

</figcaption>

</figure>

## Taxonomy

-   **Class:** Magnoliopsida
-   **Order:** Malvales
-   **Family:** Malvaceae

## Occurrence at DeLuca Preserve

-   [DeLuca Bio Blitz Species Observations](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?project_id=191727&subview=map&taxon_id=882644)

## Conservation & rarity context

-   IUCN: Not Listed
-   **Rarity note:** Rare in Florida found in in hammocks. 

## Natural history notes

A low-growing herb characterized by its slender, often flattened stems, narrow leaves, and small yellow flowers. In Florida, it is found in dry, sandy habitats such as roadsides, fields, and open disturbed areas. It grows during the warmer months and can occur as a scattered component of groundcover vegetation.

## References / links

-   [iNaturalist species page](https://www.inaturalist.org/taxa/882644-Sida-planicaulis)
-   [Local profile](https://florida.plantatlas.usf.edu/specimen/details/57638)
